fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 71 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (blocked).

spec.expr: len(recent__1) < limit or len(recent__1) > limit or (len(recent__1) == limit)

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch condition must be True (i.e., the email should be blocked), which fails with the strict > operator.
After the else branch (not blocked), the number of recent sends must be strictly less than the limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
Sanity check that recent and limit are valid before the comparison so the boundary condition is meaningful.
The returned allowed flag must be False whenever the count of recent sends is greater than or equal to the limit.
